prevalence.value: <1 per 100,000/year (mostly imported; occasional local outbreaks with Aedes albopictus)
prevalence.trend: low but increasing risk of local transmission in warmer southern regions

riskFactors: presence of Aedes albopictus in southern EU
vulnerablePopulations: residents in areas with established Aedes vectors
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical suspicion with travel history; confirmation by PCR (early) or IgM; notification per ECDC case definition

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: no screening; targeted testing of febrile travelers
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: High recovery with supportive care; public health response focuses on vector control for local transmission

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Reference lab capacity for PCR/serology; surveillance emphasis in southern member states
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Supportive management; rapid vector control (larviciding, source reduction) for local clusters
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Climate-driven expansion of Aedes vectors and rapid outbreak containment requirements
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Strong surveillance networks and coordinated outbreak response capacity across member states
